events.name: New Orleans Saints at Dallas Cowboys

headlines.description: — Derek Carr spent the offseason asking himself how he could have a faster start in a second season with the New Orleans Saints.
headlines.shortLinkText: Carr, Kamara help Saints end Cowboys' 16-game home win streak in regular season with 44-19 win

events.name: Tampa Bay Buccaneers at Detroit Lions

headlines.description: — Baker Mayfield took one step back, planted his left foot and took off for a zig-zagging, 11-yard touchdown run that gave Tampa Bay the lead for good in a playoff rematch.
headlines.shortLinkText: Mayfield runs for a TD and throws a TD pass, lifting Bucs to 20-16 win over Lions in playoff rematch

events.name: Indianapolis Colts at Green Bay Packers

headlines.description: — Malik Willis acknowledges he wasn’t ready the last time he had to start an NFL game. The Green Bay Packers quarterback knew he’d be more prepared the next time an opportunity arose.
headlines.shortLinkText: Willis thrives with help from the Packers' defense and running game in a 16-10 victory over Colts

events.name: New York Jets at Tennessee Titans

headlines.description: — Braelon Allen, the NFL's youngest player, ran for a 20-yard touchdown with 4:31 left to put Aaron Rodgers and the New York Jets ahead to stay as they beat the Tennessee Titans 24-17 on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Braelon Allen, NFL's youngest player, scores 2 TDs to help Aaron Rodgers and Jets beat Titans 24-17

events.name: Seattle Seahawks at New England Patriots

headlines.description: — Jason Myers hit a 31-yard field goal in overtime to lift the Seattle Seahawks to a 23-20 win over the New England Patriots on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jason Myers hits 31-yard field goal to lift Seahawks to a 23-20 overtime win over Patriots

events.name: New York Giants at Washington Commanders

headlines.description: — Jayden Daniels got the wind knocked out of him, missed a snap and came back like nothing went wrong. He led the Washington Commanders down...
headlines.shortLinkText: Jayden Daniels gets his 1st NFL win as Commanders beat Giants on 7 field goals by Austin Seibert

events.name: Los Angeles Chargers at Carolina Panthers

headlines.description: — Justin Herbert threw two touchdown passes to Quentin Johnston, J.K Dobbins ran for 131 yards, including a 43-yard touchdown, and the Los Angeles Chargers drubbed the Carolina Panthers 26-3 on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Justin Herbert throws 2 TDs to Quentin Johnston; Chargers hammer Panthers 26-3 to remain unbeaten

headlines.description: — Deshaun Watson ran for a touchdown, Dustin Hopkins kicked three field goals and the stingy Cleveland Browns did just enough to hold off the Jacksonville Jaguars 18-13 on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Watson runs for a TD as the Browns shut down Lawrence and the Jaguars for an 18-13 win

events.name: Las Vegas Raiders at Baltimore Ravens

headlines.description: — Gardner Minshew and the Raiders brushed aside years of frustration and criticism with a memorable performance on a splendid afternoon for underdogs.
headlines.shortLinkText: Gardner Minshew rallies Raiders past Ravens 26-23 with 3 fourth-quarter scoring drives

events.name: Los Angeles Rams at Arizona Cardinals

headlines.description: — Arizona Cardinals quarterback Kyler Murray was asked about his thoughts during that spectacular play in the first half. You know, the one where he had a Houdini-like scramble to avoid trouble before figuring out a way to pick up a big gain.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rookie Marvin Harrison Jr. catches first 2 NFL touchdowns and Cardinals roll past Rams 41-10

headlines.description: — Justin Fields and the Pittsburgh Steelers squeaked past the sputtering Denver Broncos 13-6 on Sunday with Russell Wilson reprising his bitter role as sideline spectator in the Mile High City.
headlines.shortLinkText: Justin Fields wins 2nd straight start as sub for Russell Wilson, leads Steelers past Broncos 13-6

events.name: Cincinnati Bengals at Kansas City Chiefs

headlines.description: — Harrison Butker kept making a lonely walk to midfield after each quarter Sunday to check on the direction of the wind, which tends to swirl inside Arrowhead Stadium. He did it one last time during the 2-minute warning, when his Chiefs were trailing...
headlines.shortLinkText: Butker hits a 51-yard winner for KC after penalty on Bengals safety keeps Chiefs alive

headlines.description: — In a second half where C.J. Stroud and the Houston offense struggled to move the ball, the Texans' defense stepped up Sunday night to secure a win over the Chicago Bears.
headlines.shortLinkText: Stroud throws a TD pass and Fairbairn kicks 4 field goals to help the Texans top the Bears 19-13
